_meta.note: Hash params verified from each tool's saveHash() / loadHash() source. Log10 params flagged — do not pass linear values to those fields.

constraint-stacker.title: IMBH Constraint Stacker
constraint-stacker.description: Interactive overlay of every published mass constraint on a single log-mass axis. Shows which IMBH masses remain allowed by kinematics, proper motion, pulsar timing, accretion, and N-body simultaneously.

rho.desc: Ambient density g/cm³ (linear, maps to rhoInf)

citations: Häberle et al. 2024, Nature 631, 285 (≥ 8,200 M☉ lower bound)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693 A104 (≤ 6,000 M☉ upper bound)

velocity-dispersion.title: Velocity Dispersion / M-σ Estimator
velocity-dispersion.description: What IMBH mass does the M-σ scaling relation predict for Omega Centauri? Compares predicted velocity dispersion against published estimates and adjustable overrides.

dark-cluster.title: Dark Cluster Alternative Explorer
dark-cluster.description: Models the Bañares 2025 alternative to a central IMBH — a concentrated population of stellar-mass black holes and neutron stars — and compares its kinematic and pulsar-timing signature against the single-IMBH interpretation.

gaia-dr4-forecaster.title: Gaia DR4 IMBH Constraint Forecaster
gaia-dr4-forecaster.description: Propagates Gaia DR4's documented 4.5× proper-motion precision improvement to forecast how tightly the December 2026 data release will constrain the ωCen IMBH mass via stellar kinematics. Includes a pre-registration permalink for Zenodo deposit before DR4 day.

sigma0.desc: Background cluster velocity dispersion km/s (linear, default 20)

citations: Häberle et al. 2024, Nature 631, 285 (≥ 8,200 M☉ lower bound)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693 A104 (≤ 6,000 M☉ upper bound)
ESA Cosmos: Gaia DR4 content (4.5× PM precision improvement over DR2)

anisotropy-degeneracy-explorer.title: Anisotropy Degeneracy Explorer
anisotropy-degeneracy-explorer.description: Demonstrates the kinematic IMBH degeneracy: velocity anisotropy (β > 0) and a concentrated stellar remnant cluster can both reproduce a central velocity dispersion cusp. Computes the spherical Jeans solution + Abel projection and tests detectability against the reference ωCen profile.

rrem.desc: Remnant Plummer scale radius pc (linear, default 1.0)

beta.desc: Velocity anisotropy parameter β (linear, −0.5 to 0.5, default 0)

citations: Häberle et al. 2024, Nature 631:285 (lower bound ≥ 8,200 M☉)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693:A104 (upper bound ≤ 6,000 M☉)
den Brok et al. 2023, A&A (IMBH wander + anisotropy)

pulsar-accel-mapper.title: Pulsar Acceleration Mapper
pulsar-accel-mapper.description: Maps observed line-of-sight accelerations from TRAPUM 2026 spin-period derivatives against the maximum acceleration from a central mass model (point IMBH, Plummer dark cluster, or hybrid). Tests whether a given mass is consistent with all 8 timed OC pulsars simultaneously.

model.desc: Mass model: point | plummer | hybrid (default point)

citations: Colom i Bernadich et al. 2026 (TRAPUM), arXiv:2603.21845
Chen et al. 2023, MNRAS 520:3847 (OC pulsar timing)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693 A104 (< 6,000 M☉ upper limit)

ir-excess-checker.title: IR Excess Checker
ir-excess-checker.description: Given a covering fraction f and waste-heat temperature T, computes the IR excess produced by a partial Dyson swarm and compares against WISE W3/W4 and SPHEREx survey sensitivity. Returns the f–T region current all-sky surveys exclude — the quantitative falsification backbone for the MTH hypothesis. Default is ωCen distance (5.43 kpc), Solar luminosity, T=300 K, f=0.01.
ir-excess-checker.hashNote: f is log10 (e.g. f=-2 means covering fraction 0.01). dist, temp, lstar are linear.
citations: Suazo et al. 2025 (Dyson spheres on H-R diagram), arXiv:2602.23270
Suazo et al. 2024 (Project Hephaistos), MNRAS 531, doi:10.1093/mnras/stae1186
Doré et al. 2025 (SPHEREx Science Overview), arXiv:2511.02985
Wright et al. 2014 (WISE G-star Dyson search), doi:10.1086/678977

gw-horizon-plotter.title: GW Horizon Plotter
gw-horizon-plotter.description: Characteristic strain tracks for IMRI/EMRI/IMBH-IMBH compact binaries overlaid on analytic detector sensitivity curves spanning PTA (nHz) to next-generation ground-based (kHz). Default shows both ωCen IMBH bounds (8,200 M☉ Häberle and 6,000 M☉ Bañares) at 5.43 kpc. The category-page anchor for multi-messenger GW workflows.

spin.desc: Dimensionless primary spin χ (linear, −0.998 to 0.998, default 0)

citations: Häberle et al. 2024, Nature 631:285 (≥ 8,200 M☉ lower bound)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693:A104 (≤ 6,000 M☉ upper bound)

astrometric-microlensing.title: Astrometric Microlensing Predictor
astrometric-microlensing.description: Predicts the astrometric microlensing signature a central IMBH would produce against background bulge stars, and assesses whether Gaia DR4 (≈ Dec 2026) or Roman would detect it.

qpo-mass-spin.title: QPO Mass-Spin Estimator
qpo-mass-spin.description: Constrains IMBH mass and spin from observed quasi-periodic oscillation frequencies using the ISCO orbital model and 3:2 epicyclic resonance (Török et al.). Compares against OC's constraint window.
qpo-mass-spin.hashNote: lognu is log10 of QPO frequency in Hz — pass log10 values, not linear Hz.

drake-monte-carlo.title: Drake Equation Monte Carlo
drake-monte-carlo.description: Drake equation with full uncertainty propagation via in-browser Bayesian Monte Carlo, per Sandberg, Drexler & Ord (2018). Shows why point estimates mislead and uncertainty intervals span many orders of magnitude.

fp_lo.desc: Fraction of stars with planets — lower log10 bound

fp_hi.desc: Fraction of stars with planets — upper log10 bound

ne_lo.desc: Habitable planets per system — lower log10 bound

ne_hi.desc: Habitable planets per system — upper log10 bound

fl_lo.desc: Fraction where life arises — lower log10 bound

fl_hi.desc: Fraction where life arises — upper log10 bound

fi_lo.desc: Fraction where intelligence arises — lower log10 bound

fi_hi.desc: Fraction where intelligence arises — upper log10 bound

fc_lo.desc: Fraction that communicate — lower log10 bound

fc_hi.desc: Fraction that communicate — upper log10 bound

L_lo.desc: Civilisation lifetime years — lower log10 bound

L_hi.desc: Civilisation lifetime years — upper log10 bound

great-filter.title: Great Filter Localizer
great-filter.description: Robin Hanson's Great Filter as an interactive calculator. Set survival probabilities at each step; the tool shows what fraction of the filter lies ahead and why finding microbial life on Mars would be bad news.

preset.desc: Starting preset: balanced | optimistic | pessimistic | hanson1998

you.desc: Index of the step 'we are here' (integer 0–8)

H.desc: Fraction of stars with habitable planets (linear)

citations: Hanson 1998, 'The Great Filter — Are We Almost Past It?'

radio-seti.title: Radio SETI Sensitivity
radio-seti.hashNote: ALL params are log10 — pass log10 values. e.g. eirp=13 means 10^13 W. Do NOT pass linear values.
radio-seti.description: Radiometer equation made interactive. Returns SNR, minimum detectable EIRP, and comparison to Earth's leakage and the Dec 2025 Breakthrough Listen / 3I/ATLAS benchmark.

eirp.desc: log10(transmitter EIRP W) — LOG10. e.g. 13 = 10^13 W

aeff.desc: log10(effective aperture m²) — LOG10. e.g. 3.8 = 6,310 m²

tsys.desc: log10(system temperature K) — LOG10. e.g. 1.3 = 20 K

tau.desc: log10(integration time s) — LOG10. e.g. 3.56 = 3,631 s

bekenstein-landauer.title: Bekenstein-Landauer-Lloyd Limit Explorer
bekenstein-landauer.description: The three fundamental limits on physical computation: Bekenstein bit storage, Landauer power-per-bit-erasure, Lloyd ops-from-energy. Applied to a black hole ergosphere. Labelled speculative (MTH engineering).

bz-kardashev.title: BZ / Kardashev Power Calculator
bz-kardashev.description: Blandford-Znajek power output of a spinning black hole in two modes: target a power and solve for the required magnetic field, or set the field and read off the Kardashev rung. Labelled speculative (MTH engineering).

mode.desc: inverse (solve for B given P) or forward (solve for P given B)

spin.desc: Spin parameter a* (0–0.999, default 0.9) — hash key is 'spin'

power.desc: Target power W (linear, used in inverse mode) — hash key is 'power'

B.desc: Magnetic field T (linear, used in forward mode)

evidence-ledger.title: Bayesian IMBH Evidence Ledger
evidence-ledger.description: Aggregates every published line of evidence for and against an IMBH vs dark-cluster hypothesis using a Bayesian log-odds ledger. Adjust prior percentages and per-evidence weights; the tool computes posterior odds in real time.

prior_imbh.desc: Prior probability for IMBH hypothesis (%, integer 0–100)

prior_dc.desc: Prior probability for dark-cluster hypothesis (%, integer 0–100)

prior_null.desc: Prior probability for no central mass (%, integer 0–100; all three must sum to 100)

citations: Häberle et al. 2024, Nature 631, 285 (≥ 8,200 M☉ lower bound)
Bañares-Hernández et al. 2025, A&A 693 A104 (≤ 6,000 M☉ upper bound)

detection-forecast.title: Detection Forecast
detection-forecast.description: Given the current mass tension window, computes the minimum instrument sensitivity and timeline needed to confirm or rule out an IMBH. Covers Gaia DR4, Roman Space Telescope, ELT, and MeerKAT TRAPUM.

lower.desc: Lower mass bound M☉ (linear, e.g. 8200 for Häberle 2024)

upper.desc: Upper mass bound M☉ (linear, e.g. 6000 for Bañares 2025)

observing-campaign-planner.title: Observing Campaign Planner
observing-campaign-planner.description: Resource ledger for a multi-instrument IMBH observing campaign. Select instrument (Roman, ELT, Gaia, custom), science goal, and available time; outputs required sensitivity and scheduling windows.

goal.desc: Science goal: tension | confirm | discovery | rule_out

bh-scale-comparator.title: Black Hole Scale Comparator
bh-scale-comparator.description: Places any black hole mass on a visual scale from stellar to supermassive, showing Schwarzschild radius, ISCO radius, and mass comparisons against Earth, Sun, Solar System, and Omega Centauri.

infall-survival.title: Infall Survival Calculator
infall-survival.description: Spaghettification threshold and tidal survival radius for a falling object. Computes whether a human-scale or spacecraft-scale infaller survives crossing the event horizon intact for a given IMBH mass.

height_m.desc: Object height m (linear, e.g. 1.8 for a human)

shadow-imaging.title: Black Hole Shadow Imaging
shadow-imaging.description: Computes the apparent angular diameter of a black hole's photon sphere (shadow) for any mass and distance. Shows whether EHT or ngEHT could resolve the shadow, benchmarked to M87* and Sgr A*.

kardashev-meter.title: Kardashev Meter
kardashev-meter.description: Computes a civilisation's Kardashev scale rung from current energy use and growth rate, plus the year each rung is reached. Labelled speculative (extrapolation beyond known technology).

energy_EJ.desc: Current energy use exajoules/yr (linear, e.g. 580 for Earth 2024)

energy-translator.title: Energy Unit Translator
energy-translator.description: Converts any energy or power quantity between SI units, astrophysical units (solar luminosity, Eddington), and Kardashev benchmarks. Useful for comparing civilisation power to stellar or BZ outputs.

unit.desc: Source unit key: J | kWh | Lsun | K1 | K2 | K3 | W | EJ | eV

scifi-tech-auditor.title: Sci-Fi Tech Auditor
scifi-tech-auditor.description: Rates canonical science-fiction technologies against known physics: Dyson spheres, warp drives, teleportation, Matrioshka brains, BZ ergosphere tapping, mind uploading, and MTH transcension. Shows energy requirements and physics barriers.

tech.desc: Technology slug: dyson | warp | teleport | matrioshka | btz | upload | transcension

steps.handoff: Allowed mass window becomes the prior for all subsequent tools.
M-σ prediction constrains the mass floor; output σ feeds dark-cluster comparison.
Dark-cluster model output shows which kinematic signatures are degenerate with IMBH.
Mass segregation test rules out or constrains the compact remnant population.
Timing limits set the upper bound from Bañares 2025; compare to stacker output.
Lensing is the decisive experiment — output shows Gaia DR4 / Roman detectability.
Mass window established.
Formation timeline constrains prior on mass.
M-σ prediction compared to observations.
Orbital dynamics constrains central mass.
Pulsar timing upper bound from Bañares 2025.
JWST accretion limits cross-check mass estimate.
Full uncertainty distribution on N; median and tails feed great-filter framing.
Filter placement shows how much remains ahead; output framing feeds constraint-stacker.
IMBH mass context: if a civilisation built here, what mass would it exploit?
N distribution establishes baseline civilisation density.
Filter placement; high fl feeds aestivation motivation.
Aestivation gain: if waiting pays, silence is explained without filter.
IMBH mass window: what does acting now produce?
BZ power at that mass sets the Kardashev rung achievable.
Compute limits at that mass/power; compare to aestivation gain.
Both mass bounds shown; dark-cluster lives in the gap.
Dark-cluster model tuned; residual σ profile feeds populations.
Stellar population counts constrain remnant budget.
Mass segregation check — can the remnants be this concentrated?
ISCO and ergosphere geometry at OC mass; feeds time-dilation calc.
Gravitational time dilation at ISCO feeds Hawking budget.
Evaporation timescale sets engineering deadline.
Compute limits given mass, spin, power.
Orbital compute budget — ops/s at ISCO.
BZ power at spin a; compare to superradiance channel.
Superradiance amplification; energy feeds Hawking budget.
Evaporation lifetime.
Compute limits given extracted energy.
Subjective-time speedup at ISCO.
Roadmap of open falsification tests.
Lensing detectability — Gaia DR4 / Roman.
JWST accretion flux limits.
MeerKAT TRAPUM timing sensitivity.
LISA EMRI signal: would an inspiral be detectable?

scenario-fermi-five-ways.title: Five Ways to Look for ET
